name: wifi-hawk
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``wifi-hawk`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: Capture (root): ./WifiHawkCapture -i wlan0mon -d ~/wifihawk.db  |  Plot: ./WifiHawkPlot -d ~/wifihawk.db -w resources/rules/uk-router.json -o ~/ssid-map.html -v  (also accepts PCAP via scapy sniff offline=)

examples.command: Capture (root): ./WifiHawkCapture -i wlan0mon -d ~/wifihawk.db  |  Plot: ./WifiHawkPlot -d ~/wifihawk.db -w resources/rules/uk-router.json -o ~/ssid-map.html -v  (also accepts PCAP via scapy sniff offline=)

dependencies: Google Maps Geocoding + JavaScript API keys
algorithms: scapy sniff()-based 802.11 probe-request capture with lfilter packet filter
SQLite logging of probed SSID + client MAC + timestamp
WiGLE SSID-to-GPS geolocation enrichment (API lookup with caching)
Google Maps HTML plot generation from geolocated SSIDs
Offline PCAP ingestion via scapy sniff(offline=)
produces: SQLite database of probe requests (SSID, MAC, timestamp)
novelty: Capture layer is a thin scapy wrapper (redundant vs airodump-ng probe logging). The genuinely novel part is the downstream pipeline: WiGLE SSID-to-GPS enrichment of passively observed probe requests, UK-ISP-router default-SSID whitelist filtering, and Google Maps visualization aimed at physically geolocating a target's previously-joined networks — a probe-request-driven physical-locale inference workflow not present in airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy baseline.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
